articles.title: Xabi Alonso Signs Four-Year Deal as Chelsea Manager
articles.summary: Chelsea have officially confirmed Xabi Alonso as their new manager on a four-year contract starting July 1, 2026. The Spanish coach arrives after a brief spell at Real Madrid and his famous unbeaten Bundesliga title run with Bayer Leverkusen. He becomes Chelsea's fifth permanent manager under the BlueCo ownership group.
articles.content:
Chelsea have found their man. Xabi Alonso has been officially appointed as Chelsea manager on a four-year contract, with his role beginning on July 1, 2026. The announcement came just hours after Chelsea's 1-0 FA Cup final defeat to Manchester City at Wembley, giving the club's fanbase something to look forward to after a painful afternoon.

Alonso, 44, is a name that commands instant respect in football. He famously led Bayer Leverkusen to an unbeaten Bundesliga title, becoming one of the most celebrated young coaches in Europe. His time at Real Madrid lasted only half a season before he was let go in January, but Chelsea clearly saw that as a blip rather than a pattern. He visited London last week to finalise terms on the four-year deal.

The appointment is significant for several reasons. Chelsea have spent £1.8 billion on signings since BlueCo took over in 2022 with very little consistency to show for it. Alonso reportedly secured assurances that he will have a real say over transfers, something previous managers including Mauricio Pochettino struggled to get. There is already strong internal hope that Alonso can revitalise Cole Palmer, whose form has dipped over the last 18 months. The comparison being made inside the club is with Florian Wirtz, who flourished under Alonso at Leverkusen before his £116 million move to Liverpool.

Alonso said he was filled with "immense pride" to take the job, and that his conversations with ownership made it clear they share the same ambition: "We want to build a team capable of competing consistently at the highest level and fighting for trophies." After a season that saw Chelsea finish ninth in the Premier League and lose the FA Cup final, the pressure to deliver will be immense. But if anyone has earned the right to demand patience and proper backing, it is Xabi Alonso.
keyPoints: Xabi Alonso has signed a four-year deal as Chelsea manager starting July 1, 2026
He is Chelsea's fifth permanent manager under the BlueCo ownership
Alonso reportedly secured assurances over transfer decisions
Chelsea have spent £1.8 billion on players since 2022 with little stability
There is strong internal hope he can unlock Cole Palmer's best form

articles.title: Keanu Reeves Joins Stop-Motion Samurai Film Hidari
articles.summary: Keanu Reeves has signed on to voice the lead character Jingoro in Hidari, the highly anticipated feature film from Japanese director Masashi Kawamura — marking Reeves's first involvement in an original Japanese production. The announcement was made today at the Annecy Animation Showcase at the Cannes Film Festival. Hidari is a 90-minute stop-motion action film expanding on Kawamura's 2023 proof-of-concept short, which has racked up nearly 5 million YouTube views.
articles.content:
Keanu Reeves is heading to Edo-era Japan. The John Wick star has officially signed on to voice Jingoro, the lead character in Hidari, the long-awaited stop-motion feature from Japanese director Masashi Kawamura. The news dropped this morning at the Annecy Animation Showcase, part of the Cannes Film Festival.

If you have not seen the short film that started all of this, go watch it now. Kawamura released a five-and-a-half-minute proof-of-concept short in 2023 that blew up online, amassing close to 5 million YouTube views. It showed a legendary Edo-era craftsman named Jingoro Hidari, a mysterious real-world figure whose very existence is disputed by historians, taking revenge on those who betrayed him. The twist is his weapon: a series of lethal mechanical prosthetic arms he forges himself after losing his right arm. The short looked unlike anything else in animation, blending the visual intensity of anime with hand-carved wooden puppets animated frame by frame.

The feature film is set to run 90 minutes and will go much deeper into Jingoro's world. Betrayed during the reconstruction of Edo Castle, he loses his father figure, his fiancée, and his right arm. He then turns his extraordinary carpentry skills toward vengeance, accompanied by his loyal companion the Sleeping Cat. The film weaves in real historical events including the Keian Uprising and the Great Fire of Meireki alongside fictional elements inspired by works traditionally attributed to Jingoro Hidari.

For Reeves, this is genuinely new territory. It marks his first involvement in an original Japanese production. He has voiced animated characters before, playing Duke Caboom in Pixar's Toy Story 4 and Shadow the Hedgehog in Sonic the Hedgehog 3, but Hidari is a different beast entirely. Kawamura said he was thrilled when Reeves came on board, noting that Reeves is not just lending his voice but actively helping to shape and expand the world of the film.

Hidari is being produced by Noriko Matsumoto at dwarf studios, the Tokyo-based outfit behind Netflix's beloved Rilakkuma series, in collaboration with Kawamura's own studio Whatever and stop-motion specialists TECARAT. The film is currently slated for completion in 2029. No distributor has been announced yet, but with Keanu Reeves attached and nearly 5 million views already earned before a single frame of the feature exists, Hidari is arguably the most exciting animation project in development right now.
keyPoints: Keanu Reeves will voice Jingoro, the lead character in the stop-motion feature Hidari
This is Reeves's first involvement in an original Japanese production
The announcement was made at the Annecy Animation Showcase at the 79th Cannes Film Festival
Hidari is based on director Masashi Kawamura's 2023 viral short film, which has nearly 5 million YouTube views
The 90-minute feature is slated for completion in 2029

articles.title: McGregor vs Holloway 2 Set for UFC 329 in Las Vegas July 11
articles.summary: UFC CEO Dana White has officially confirmed that Conor McGregor will return to the octagon on July 11, 2026, to face Max Holloway in the main event of UFC 329 at the T-Mobile Arena in Las Vegas. The welterweight rematch is McGregor's first fight since breaking his leg in July 2021, and it comes nearly 13 years after their original 2013 encounter. The fight will air live on Paramount+ as the centrepiece of the UFC's annual International Fight Week.
articles.content:
It is finally happening. Conor McGregor is coming back. UFC CEO Dana White made it official during Saturday's UFC Fight Night 276 broadcast on Paramount+, confirming that McGregor will headline UFC 329 against Max Holloway on July 11, 2026, at the T-Mobile Arena in Las Vegas.

For those who need the backstory: McGregor last set foot in the octagon in July 2021, when he broke his lower leg during a trilogy fight with Dustin Poirier. That injury ended the night and started what became nearly five years on the sidelines. A planned return against Michael Chandler fell apart when McGregor withdrew with a broken toe, and an 18-month suspension for whereabouts failures added more time to the wait. He comes back now with a 22-6 professional record and a lot to prove.

Holloway is no soft touch as a comeback opponent. The former UFC featherweight champion holds a 27-9 record and is widely regarded as one of the best fighters of his generation. He is coming off a decision loss to Charles Oliveira in a BMF title bout at UFC 326 in March 2026, but that setback barely dents his reputation. This rematch also carries genuine personal stakes: the two first met in August 2013, with McGregor winning by unanimous decision, and Holloway spent 13 consecutive wins after that defeat rebuilding himself into a champion.

The fight is scheduled for five rounds at the welterweight limit of 170 pounds, a step up from the featherweight 145-pound limit at which they first met. McGregor's team reportedly pushed for a three-round contest during negotiations, but the five-round distance was confirmed for the Paramount+ pay-per-view main card. The supporting lineup is stacked too, with Paddy Pimblett versus Benoit Saint-Denis in a lightweight clash, Cody Garbrandt versus Adrian Yanez at bantamweight, and Robert Whittaker taking on Nikita Krylov at light heavyweight.

The social media back-and-forth has already started. When Holloway posted about his July 11 return, McGregor fired back in classic fashion. Whether that bulletin-board energy translates inside the cage is the whole question. McGregor has won only once in his last four UFC appearances, while Holloway has stayed far more active and sharp across multiple weight classes. July 11 in Las Vegas is going to be loud.
keyPoints: Conor McGregor returns to the UFC for the first time since July 2021
McGregor faces Max Holloway in the UFC 329 main event on July 11, 2026
The welterweight bout takes place at the T-Mobile Arena in Las Vegas during International Fight Week
The fight airs live on Paramount+ and is scheduled for five rounds at 170 pounds
Their first meeting was in 2013, with McGregor winning by unanimous decision

articles.title: Bulgaria's Dara Wins Eurovision 2026 With 'Bangaranga'
articles.summary: Bulgaria's Dara made history on Saturday night, winning the Eurovision Song Contest 2026 in Vienna with her song 'Bangaranga' — the country's first ever Eurovision victory. Dara dominated the scoreboard with 516 points, topping both the jury and the public televote. It is the first time any artist has swept both votes simultaneously since Salvador Sobral won for Portugal back in 2017.
articles.content:
History was made at Vienna's Wiener Stadthalle on Saturday night. Bulgaria's Dara won the 70th Eurovision Song Contest with her track 'Bangaranga', becoming Bulgaria's first ever Eurovision champion. The win came as something of a surprise. Bulgaria was not among the bookmakers' favourites going into the final, but the song's energy and charm won over both the professional juries and millions of televoting fans across Europe and beyond.

The numbers tell the full story. Dara finished with 516 points, 204 of which came from the juries (first place) and 312 from the public vote (also first place). That made her the first artist to win both streams of voting simultaneously since Salvador Sobral claimed the trophy for Portugal nine years ago. Israel's Noam Bettan finished second with his song 'Michelle', performed in French, Hebrew and English, while Romania's Alexandra Căpitănescu came in third with 'Choke Me'. Australia's Delta Goodrem placed fourth with her power ballad 'Eclipse', described as the country's strongest Eurovision result in several years.

Dara, whose real name is Darina Yotova, is 27 years old and previously appeared on the Bulgarian edition of The X Factor in 2015, reaching the final. She co-wrote 'Bangaranga' alongside celebrated Greek producer Dimitris Kontopoulos, Romanian hitmaker Monoir, and Norwegian songwriter Anne Judith Stokke Wik. The song blends dance-pop energy with bhangra-adjacent rhythms, and its 'Welcome to the riot!' refrain became one of the most quoted lines of the contest week. Bulgaria had actually sat out three consecutive Eurovision contests between 2023 and 2025, making the comeback win even more dramatic.

At a press conference after the win, Dara revealed she nearly did not enter at all. She said she had 'doubt and anxiety' about competing and credited her husband with pushing her to take part. 'He was like, you need to go right now to Eurovision, right now, pick up your phone, tell them you're going,' she said. Her win means Bulgaria will host Eurovision 2027 in the capital, Sofia — and after years of near misses including Kristian Kostov's runner-up finish at Eurovision 2017, the whole country finally has its moment in the spotlight.
keyPoints: Bulgaria's Dara won Eurovision 2026 in Vienna with 'Bangaranga', the country's first ever win
Dara scored 516 points, topping both the jury vote and the public televote
She is the first artist to win both voting streams simultaneously since 2017
Israel's Noam Bettan finished second, Romania's Alexandra Căpitănescu third, and Australia's Delta Goodrem fourth
Bulgaria will now host Eurovision 2027 in Sofia

articles.title: Semenyo's 72nd-Minute Backheel Wins Manchester City the FA Cup
articles.summary: Antoine Semenyo scored one of the great FA Cup final goals as Manchester City beat Chelsea 1-0 at Wembley to complete a domestic cup double. Semenyo, a January signing from AFC Bournemouth, flicked a backheel into the far corner in the 72nd minute to settle a tight and cagey final. It is Manchester City's eighth FA Cup title and Pep Guardiola's 20th trophy in English football.
articles.content:
One flick. One goal. One trophy. Antoine Semenyo wrote his name into FA Cup final history on Saturday with a backheel finish so audacious it barely seemed real. Manchester City beat Chelsea 1-0 at Wembley to win their eighth FA Cup and complete a domestic cup double under Pep Guardiola.

The goal arrived in the 72nd minute and it was the only moment of genuine attacking quality in a tight, cagey final. Bernardo Silva found Erling Haaland, who turned provider with a precise pass into the area. With his back to goal and under pressure, Semenyo pivoted and flicked a backheel into the bottom corner past Chelsea goalkeeper Robert Sanchez. It was the kind of finish that will be replayed for years. Semenyo himself said: "Just tried to improvise the best I could. It went into the bottom corner, so can't complain."

For Guardiola, it is a 20th trophy in English football, achieved in what could still be his final season at the Etihad Stadium. Manchester City had already won the Carabao Cup earlier in the season, beating Arsenal in that final, and they now have their sights on Arsenal's Premier League lead in the final two games of the season with a potential Treble still alive. Rayan Cherki, introduced at half time in place of Omar Marmoush, played a key role in the build-up to the winner after an impressive second-half cameo.

For Chelsea it was another Wembley heartbreak. This was their fourth consecutive FA Cup final defeat and their seventh domestic final in a row without lifting the trophy. They pushed hard in the final stages but never truly threatened an equaliser. The defeat also means Chelsea now face a battle to qualify for European football through the Premier League in the final week of the season, having missed out on the Europa League route that a cup win would have provided.
keyPoints: Manchester City beat Chelsea 1-0 in the FA Cup final at Wembley
Antoine Semenyo scored the winner with a backheel finish in the 72nd minute
It is City's eighth FA Cup and Guardiola's 20th trophy in English football
Man City completed a domestic cup double having already won the Carabao Cup
Chelsea have now lost four consecutive FA Cup finals
articles.wikiSearchQuery: 2026 FA Cup Final Manchester City Chelsea

articles.title: AI Is Helping Dentists Push Treatments You Probably Don't Need
articles.summary: A new investigation, spotlighted in former Wall Street Journal columnist Joanna Stern's book "I Am Not a Robot: My Year Using AI to Do (Almost) Everything," reveals that AI diagnostic tools like Pearl AI and Overjet are being used by dentists to upsell patients on unnecessary procedures. Stern discovered the pattern firsthand when a dentist using Pearl AI told her she needed four sessions of periodontal treatment after the AI flagged heavy plaque — a finding that turned out to be far more alarming than her actual dental health warranted. Stern says the problem is widespread, warning that it is "happening almost everywhere."
articles.content:
You go in for a routine check-up. The dentist pulls up an AI scan, points at some ominous-looking areas on your X-ray and tells you that you need an expensive course of treatment. You trust the technology. You book the appointments. But what if the AI was nudging the dentist to oversell?

That is the uncomfortable picture painted by a new investigation tied to Joanna Stern's book "I Am Not a Robot: My Year Using AI to Do (Almost) Everything." Stern, a former Wall Street Journal tech columnist, discovered the issue firsthand during a routine dentist visit. The office used a tool called Pearl AI, which markets itself as catching "37 percent more disease and deliver 24 percent more care to patients in need." Pearl AI flagged significant plaque build-up, and the dentist used that finding to recommend a full course of periodontal treatment across four separate sessions. Stern started digging and found it was not an isolated case. Speaking on The New York Times' "Hard Fork" podcast, Stern said: "It's happening almost everywhere."

Pearl AI and Overjet are two of the most widely used AI diagnostic tools in dentistry right now. The concern is not that these tools are entirely wrong — AI genuinely can catch things human eyes miss. The problem, as Stern argues, is the incentive structure around them. When an AI flags a potential issue, it creates a moment of pressure. The patient sees something alarming on a screen, the dentist has a reason to recommend treatment, and the financial upside for the practice is real. The gap between a genuine diagnosis and a profitable one gets blurry fast.

This matters because dentistry has always had an upselling problem, long before AI arrived. Studies have repeatedly shown wide variation in treatment recommendations between dentists examining the same patient. AI was supposed to bring consistency and accuracy to that process. Instead, critics say some practices are using it as a more convincing sales tool. The technology looks authoritative on a screen in a way that a dentist's subjective opinion does not.

For patients, the practical takeaway is simple: if your dentist recommends a significant treatment after an AI-assisted scan, it is worth getting a second opinion before booking anything. Pearl AI's own marketing promises better detection rates, but independent research suggests these tools still produce false positives, and the financial incentives inside dental practices do not always push in the patient's favour. AI in dentistry is not going away, but knowing how it is being used is the first step to not getting taken for a ride.
keyPoints: AI tools Pearl AI and Overjet are being used in dental offices to flag issues that lead to unnecessary treatment recommendations
Joanna Stern uncovered the problem firsthand when Pearl AI flagged her for four sessions of periodontal treatment she likely did not need
Stern says the upselling problem is "happening almost everywhere" in dental practices using AI diagnostics
The issue lies in the incentive structure: AI findings give dentists a convincing, screen-based reason to recommend expensive procedures
Patients are advised to seek a second opinion before agreeing to significant dental treatment recommended after an AI scan

articles.title: Michael Carrick Gets the Manchester United Job Permanently
articles.summary: Manchester United have confirmed Michael Carrick as permanent head coach after his stunning interim spell turned the club's season around. Carrick took over in January following the exit of Ruben Amorim and guided United back into the UEFA Champions League. He has signed a two-year deal with the option of a further year.
articles.content:
It is official. Manchester United have handed Michael Carrick the permanent head coach job, ending months of speculation about who would lead the club next season. The 44-year-old took over as interim manager in January after Ruben Amorim's rocky reign came to an acrimonious end, and what followed exceeded almost everyone's expectations.

The numbers tell the story. In 15 games in charge, Carrick won 10 and drew three, picking up wins against Manchester City, Liverpool, Arsenal and Chelsea along the way. Most importantly, he secured Champions League football for next season with three matches to spare, a result that could bring the club a potential £100 million boost in revenue.

Carrick has signed a two-year contract until June 2028 with the option of a further year. The club's director of football Jason Wilcox and CEO Omar Berrada recommended Carrick for the role, with co-owner Sir Jim Ratcliffe approving the appointment. Winger Amad Diallo summed up the dressing room mood, saying the players would be happy if Carrick stayed: "He's that manager who has a good relationship with every player."

Carrick himself said he was "honoured and excited" to take charge permanently, adding: "This club means everything to me. We have momentum now, and I can't wait to build on it." For a club that has cycled through manager after manager since Sir Alex Ferguson retired in 2013, a head coach with this level of player support and instant results feels like a genuine turning point.
keyPoints: Manchester United have confirmed Michael Carrick as permanent head coach
Carrick took over as interim in January after Ruben Amorim left the club
He won 10 of his first 15 games and secured Champions League qualification
He has signed a two-year deal until June 2028 with an option for a third year
Players including Amad Diallo publicly backed Carrick to stay

articles.title: Tom Francis Auditions for James Bond at Just 26
articles.summary: Tom Francis, the Olivier Award-winning star of Broadway and West End's Sunset Boulevard, has officially auditioned to become the next James Bond for Amazon MGM Studios. At 26 and relatively fresh to screens, he is one of the most surprising names in the race to replace Daniel Craig as 007. The casting process is being led by Oscar-nominated casting director Nina Gold, and Francis is far from the only contender — but he may be the most intriguing one.
articles.content:
The next James Bond search just got a lot more interesting. Tom Francis, the 26-year-old British actor best known for starring opposite Nicole Scherzinger in Jamie Lloyd's unconventional revival of Andrew Lloyd Webber's Sunset Boulevard, has officially auditioned for the role of 007, according to Variety.

Francis is a stage star first and foremost. He won the Laurence Olivier Award for Best Actor in a Musical for his performance as Joe Gillis in Sunset Boulevard, and picked up a Tony Award nomination when the production transferred to Broadway. One of the show's standout moments saw him perform the title number while walking through New York's theater district. That combination of physical presence, charisma, and dramatic range has clearly caught Amazon MGM Studios' attention.

On screen his CV is thin but well-connected. His only film credit so far is a small role in George Clooney's Jay Kelly — and notably, that film was produced by Amy Pascal and David Heyman, who are now overseeing the Bond franchise. He is next set to appear in Peter Berg's World War II sports drama The Mosquito Bowl, alongside Nicholas Galitzine and Bill Skarsgård. Amazon MGM Studios is reportedly seeking British actors under 30, someone who can carry the franchise for at least a decade, which aligns perfectly with Francis's profile.

He is far from alone in the running. Other names swirling around the Bond 26 search include Jacob Elordi, Aaron Taylor-Johnson, and Callum Turner, though none of those have been confirmed as having actually sat down for an audition the way Francis has. The casting process is being run by Nina Gold, the Oscar-nominated casting director behind Game of Thrones, Star Wars: The Force Awakens, and Conclave. No official release date for Bond 26 has been set yet, but sources suggest the announcement of the new 007 could come as late as early 2027.
keyPoints: Tom Francis, 26, has officially auditioned to play James Bond for Amazon MGM Studios
He won an Olivier Award and received a Tony nomination for Sunset Boulevard opposite Nicole Scherzinger
His only film role so far is in George Clooney's Jay Kelly, produced by the same team now running Bond
Casting director Nina Gold is leading the search, reportedly focused on British actors under 30
Other rumoured contenders include Jacob Elordi, Aaron Taylor-Johnson, and Callum Turner

articles.title: Ronda Rousey Taps Gina Carano in 17 Seconds Then Retires Again
articles.summary: Ronda Rousey returned to MMA at MVP MMA 1 on Netflix last night and submitted Gina Carano via armbar in just 17 seconds at the Intuit Dome in Inglewood, California. Rousey, now 39, had not fought since December 2016 while Carano had been out of competition since 2009, and Carano made the remarkable effort of losing 100 pounds over roughly 18 months just to make the fight happen. After the win, Rousey confirmed she is going straight back into retirement.
articles.content:
Ronda Rousey stepped into a cage for the first time in nearly a decade last night and reminded everyone exactly who she is. At MVP MMA 1, the debut MMA card from Most Valuable Promotions streamed live on Netflix, Rousey needed exactly 17 seconds to submit Gina Carano via armbar in front of a packed Intuit Dome in Inglewood, California.

The fight barely lasted long enough to blink. Rousey shot for a takedown within three seconds of the opening bell, landed in mount, and locked up the armbar before Carano could even think about defending. The tap came almost immediately. Only two total strikes were thrown across the entire fight. Carano, to her credit, was gracious afterward: "Getting in the cage was a victory," she said, adding that she had wanted the fight to last longer and had felt genuinely ready.

The backstory here is remarkable. Carano, 44, had not competed since suffering a knockout loss to Cris Cyborg in 2009 in a bout for the Strikeforce women's featherweight title. To make this fight happen, she said she lost 100 pounds over roughly a year and a half, thanking Rousey for waiting patiently while she got herself back into fighting shape. Rousey, 39, was the UFC's inaugural women's bantamweight champion and a genuine pioneer of the sport before suffering back-to-back knockout losses to Holly Holm and Amanda Nunes that ended her UFC run in 2016.

Most Valuable Promotions is Jake Paul's combat sports company, which has previously made waves with boxing events. MVP MMA 1 marked its first major push into mixed martial arts, and using Netflix as its broadcast home meant the event was available to millions of subscribers worldwide without an extra pay-per-view fee. The full card also featured Francis Ngannou knocking out Philipe Lins in round one and Mike Perry stopping a bloodied Nate Diaz via corner stoppage in round two, making for a genuinely stacked night.

As for what comes next, the answer for Rousey is nothing. After the win, the 39-year-old confirmed she is heading straight back into retirement. So this was it: one final performance, one vintage finish, and then she walked away on her own terms. It was the perfect full stop on one of the most influential careers in combat sports history.
keyPoints: Ronda Rousey submitted Gina Carano via armbar in 17 seconds at MVP MMA 1 on Netflix
The event took place at the Intuit Dome in Inglewood, California on May 16, 2026
Carano had not fought since 2009 and lost 100 pounds to make the fight happen
Rousey confirmed after the win that she is returning to retirement
MVP MMA 1 was Most Valuable Promotions' first ever MMA card, streamed on Netflix

articles.title: Eric Schmidt Gets Booed at Arizona Graduation While Pitching AI
articles.summary: Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt was jeered repeatedly by University of Arizona graduates on Friday, May 16, 2026, as he delivered a commencement address about artificial intelligence and the future of work. The boos grew loudest when Schmidt acknowledged student fears about AI taking jobs, telling the crowd "I can hear you" as the shouts intensified. Students had also organised ahead of the event over separate sexual assault allegations made against Schmidt, which his legal team denies.
articles.content:
Graduation day is supposed to be a celebration. For former Google CEO Eric Schmidt at the University of Arizona on Friday, it was something else entirely. As Schmidt stepped up to deliver the commencement address to nearly 10,000 graduates from the Class of 2026, he was met with boos that broke out repeatedly throughout his speech.

The loudest jeers came when Schmidt turned to the topic of AI. He paused mid-speech and addressed the crowd directly, saying: "I know what many of you are feeling about that. I can hear you. There is a fear." He told the graduates he understood they felt they were "inheriting a mess" they did not create, and called those fears "rational." But he pushed back, arguing that the real question was not whether AI would shape the world but whether graduates would shape AI. The University of Arizona invited Schmidt because of his contributions to tech and innovation, with a spokesperson pointing to his role in building Google into one of the world's most influential companies.

The friction was not just about jobs. Student advocacy groups, including FORCE (Feminists Organised to Resist Create and Empower), Students for Socialism, the Women and Gender Resource Center and Pride Alliance, had circulated a petition with over 1,260 signatures before the ceremony calling for Schmidt's speech to be cancelled. The groups distributed flyers and called on attendees to boo or turn their backs. Their concerns centred on a 2025 sexual assault lawsuit filed by Schmidt's former business partner Michelle Ritter, as well as allegations that Schmidt was named in the Epstein files. Schmidt's legal team has firmly denied the allegations, calling them "fabricated," and in March a judge ordered the suit into arbitration.

The moment captures something bigger than one uncomfortable graduation night. Schmidt, who led Google from 2001 to 2011 and helped build the modern internet, now finds himself personally identified with the very disruption he helped create. Meanwhile, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang gave a graduation speech at Carnegie Mellon the same week and received a far warmer reception, striking a positive tone about AI creating new opportunities. The contrast says a lot about how different audiences are processing the same technological shift right now.
keyPoints: Eric Schmidt was booed repeatedly at the University of Arizona's Class of 2026 commencement on May 16
Boos peaked when Schmidt discussed AI and job fears, with Schmidt pausing to say "I can hear you"
Student groups organised the protest ahead of the event, collecting over 1,260 petition signatures
Protests also targeted a 2025 sexual assault lawsuit against Schmidt, which his legal team denies
Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang spoke at Carnegie Mellon the same week to a much warmer reception

articles.title: Bobby Prince's Doom Soundtrack Joins the US Library of Congress
articles.summary: The soundtrack to the original 1993 Doom, composed by Bobby Prince, has been inducted into the United States Library of Congress's National Recording Registry as part of the Class of 2026. It is only the third video game soundtrack ever to receive this honour, joining the Super Mario Bros. theme and the Minecraft: Volume Alpha score. Doom's riff-heavy, metal-inspired music now sits on the same list as Taylor Swift's 1989 and Beyoncé's 'Single Ladies (Put a Ring on It)'.
articles.content:
Hell has officially frozen over. The soundtrack to the original 1993 Doom has been added to the United States Library of Congress's National Recording Registry, cementing its place as one of the most culturally significant pieces of music in gaming history.

The Doom soundtrack, composed by Bobby Prince, is one of 25 recordings selected this year from more than 3,000 public nominations. Acting Librarian of Congress Robert R. Newlen described the inductees as "audio treasures worthy of preservation for all time based on their cultural, historical or aesthetic importance." With Doom and the other 2026 additions included, the National Recording Registry now holds exactly 700 titles total. The 2026 class is a wild mix: Doom sits alongside Taylor Swift's 1989, Beyoncé's Single Ladies (Put a Ring on It), Weezer's Blue Album, The Go-Go's Beauty and the Beat, and the original Mambo No. 5 by Pérez Prado and His Orchestra.

Bobby Prince was a freelance composer and a practicing lawyer with a deep interest in MIDI technology. For the Doom score, Prince drew inspiration from a pile of CDs loaned to him by Doom's chief designer, John Romero. That collection included major works by Alice in Chains, Pantera, and Metallica. Working within the brutal limitations of 1993 sound card drivers, Prince used his MIDI knowledge to assign in-game sound effects to different frequencies so they could cut through the music cleanly. He reportedly wrote all of the music before id Software had even finished building the game's levels.

This is only the third time video game music has made the Registry. The Super Mario Bros. theme was the first, inducted in 2023, and the Minecraft: Volume Alpha soundtrack followed. Doom represents something different from both of those entries. Where Mario offered cheerful platformer melodies and Minecraft delivered ambient calm, Doom brought dark, metal-driven aggression into America's cultural archive. It is a recognition that game music is no longer treated as background noise but as a genuine art form worth preserving forever.
keyPoints: Bobby Prince's 1993 Doom soundtrack has been inducted into the US Library of Congress's National Recording Registry
Doom is only the third video game soundtrack ever added, after the Super Mario Bros. theme and Minecraft: Volume Alpha
The 2026 class of 25 recordings brings the Registry's total to exactly 700 titles
Prince drew inspiration from Alice in Chains, Pantera, and Metallica CDs loaned by Doom designer John Romero
Doom's co-inductees include Taylor Swift's 1989, Beyoncé's Single Ladies, and Weezer's Blue Album

articles.title: Kylie Minogue Accidentally Confirms Her 2027 Anniversary Tour
articles.summary: Kylie Minogue has let slip that a 40th anniversary tour is in the works for 2027, saying "I'm probably not meant to say this, but yes" when asked about the plans. The tour would mark four decades since Minogue launched her pop career in 1987 while still appearing in the Australian soap opera Neighbours. The news lands just days before her three-part Netflix documentary series KYLIE premieres on 20 May.
articles.content:
Kylie Minogue has a habit of being refreshingly honest, and she did it again this week. Asked about plans for a 40th anniversary tour, she admitted: "I'm probably not meant to say this, but yes." Consider that the most glamorous accidental announcement in pop right now.

The tour would land in 2027, which marks 40 years since Minogue first burst onto the scene. She launched her pop career in 1987 while still starring in the Australian TV soap Neighbours, with a cover of "The Loco-Motion" that went straight to number one in Australia. Few could have predicted that a soap actress doing a cover version would become one of the most enduring pop stars on the planet.

Four decades on, Minogue has sold over 80 million records worldwide. Her 2001 single "Can't Get You Out of My Head" became a number one across Europe and remains one of pop's most recognisable songs. Her most recent album, Tension, gave her another huge cultural moment with the viral hit "Padam Padam" in 2023, proving she is nowhere near done.

The timing of this slip is no accident. Minogue's three-part Netflix documentary series, simply titled KYLIE, premieres on 20 May and was directed by Emmy and BAFTA Award winner Michael Harte, the team behind the acclaimed BECKHAM and WHAM! documentaries. The series features contributions from Dannii Minogue, Jason Donovan, Nick Cave, and Pete Waterman, and draws on personal archives, home movies, and new interviews. It also covers her 2005 breast cancer diagnosis and the personal challenges she has faced across five decades in the public eye.

The anniversary tour idea is being compared to Taylor Swift's Eras Tour and Madonna's Celebration tour, in that it would span different chapters of Minogue's career rather than focusing on a single album. With that much history to pull from, spanning dance-pop, disco, electronic music, and mainstream pop, it could be something genuinely spectacular. For now, talks are underway and nothing is officially locked in. But when Kylie says yes, you believe her.
keyPoints: Kylie Minogue confirmed a 40th anniversary tour is being planned for 2027
She launched her career in 1987 with a number one cover of The Loco-Motion in Australia while starring in Neighbours
Minogue has sold over 80 million records across five decades
Her Netflix documentary KYLIE, directed by Michael Harte, premieres on 20 May 2026
The tour concept is inspired by career-spanning shows like Taylor Swift's Eras Tour

articles.title: Svitolina Beats Gauff in Rome Thriller to Win Third Italian Open
articles.summary: Elina Svitolina claimed her third Italian Open title on May 16, defeating Coco Gauff 6-4, 6-7(3), 6-2 in a nearly three-hour final in Rome. The win is Svitolina's 20th career title and extends her perfect record to 8-0 in clay-court finals. It also sets her up as a serious French Open contender with Roland Garros just weeks away.
articles.content:
Elina Svitolina is a Roman empress again. The Ukrainian world No. 8 defeated Coco Gauff 6-4, 6-7(3), 6-2 on May 16 at the Foro Italico to claim her third Italian Open crown. The match lasted nearly three hours and had everything: early drama, a tiebreak that slipped away from Svitolina, and then a dominant final set that left no doubt about who the better player was on the day.

Gauff actually started the brighter of the two. She broke Svitolina and held to love, and even had the chance to go 5-2 up in the first set. But Svitolina fought off three break points and reeled off four straight games to take the opener. The second set was tighter, with Gauff struggling on serve but grinding her way to a tiebreak, which she won 7-3 to level the match. Then the decider belonged to Svitolina completely. She broke Gauff's serve twice and closed it out with authority.

This was no ordinary title run. Svitolina beat world No. 2 Elena Rybakina and world No. 4 Iga Swiatek on her way to the final, saving 16 of 20 break points against Rybakina alone. That kind of mental toughness under pressure is what separates good players from great ones. For Gauff, it was a second straight Italian Open final loss, having fallen to Jasmine Paolini in 2025. The 22-year-old was gracious in defeat, saying it had been a great two weeks and that she felt momentum building toward the French Open.

For Svitolina, the trophy was deeply personal. She last won in Rome in 2018, meaning this was an eight-year wait for a title at the Foro Italico. On court after the win, she revealed she had told her coach she wanted to reach 20 career titles before retiring. That goal is now ticked off. With her clay-court finals record now a perfect 8-0 and Roland Garros starting on May 24, Svitolina heads to Paris as one of the most dangerous players in the draw.
keyPoints: Svitolina beat Gauff 6-4, 6-7(3), 6-2 in nearly three hours to win the 2026 Italian Open
It is Svitolina's third Italian Open title, adding to her wins in 2017 and 2018
The win is her 20th career title and keeps her clay-court finals record at a perfect 8-0
Gauff loses the Rome final for the second straight year, having lost to Paolini in 2025
Svitolina heads to the French Open on May 24 as a major contender

articles.title: Amazon Forces AI Quotas, Employees Game MeshClaw for Personal Tasks
articles.summary: Amazon set a target requiring more than 80% of its developers to use its internal AI tool MeshClaw every week, then tracked their token consumption on internal leaderboards. Employees responded by using MeshClaw for personal and pointless tasks just to hit the numbers, a practice now being called 'tokenmaxxing'. The backlash reveals a growing crisis of trust between big tech companies and their workers over how AI adoption is actually measured.
articles.content:
Amazon wanted its engineers to go all-in on AI. So it set a target requiring more than 80% of its developers to use the company's in-house AI agent, MeshClaw, every single week. Then it started tracking everyone's AI token consumption on an internal leaderboard. What happened next was completely predictable.

Employees started using MeshClaw for everything except real work. According to a Financial Times report, workers began firing off pointless, low-value tasks through MeshClaw purely to pump up their token numbers. The practice has a name now: 'tokenmaxxing'. One anonymous worker put it bluntly, saying companies that use what they called "brain-dead metrics" to judge people should expect to get gamed right back.

Here is the awkward part. Amazon officially said usage statistics would not factor into performance evaluations. But multiple employees told the Financial Times they believed managers were watching the data closely anyway. One described feeling "so much pressure to use these tools." Another said the tracking created "perverse incentives." When people think their jobs are on the line, they do what they have to do.

Amazon is not alone in this mess. Meta built an internal leaderboard called 'Claudeonomics' that ranked roughly 85,000 employees by token consumption. Microsoft's developer division president told managers that AI use was "no longer optional." The bigger problem is the money sitting behind all of this. Combined 2026 capital spending from Amazon, Microsoft, Alphabet, and Meta is tracking between $650 billion and $700 billion, with some Wall Street projections exceeding $1 trillion for 2027. If a meaningful chunk of the AI consumption numbers propping up those investments is fake, somebody has a very big problem. Experts are already calling this a textbook case of Goodhart's Law: when a metric becomes a target, it stops being a useful metric.
keyPoints: Amazon required over 80% of developers to use MeshClaw weekly and tracked usage on internal leaderboards
Employees responded by 'tokenmaxxing': running pointless tasks through MeshClaw just to hit their numbers
Amazon said usage stats would not affect performance reviews, but employees did not believe it
Meta and Microsoft have faced the same problem with their own AI usage mandates
Combined big tech AI spending in 2026 tops $650 billion, making inflated usage data a serious financial risk

articles.title: Bad Bunny Launches Zara Collab Under His Benito Antonio Brand
articles.summary: Bad Bunny has teamed up with Spanish retail giant Zara to drop a new clothing collection under his personal brand, Benito Antonio. The collaboration was officially announced on Zara's Instagram and marks a major fashion moment for the global superstar. It is one of the most high-profile celebrity-brand apparel partnerships of the year.
articles.content:
Bad Bunny is not just running the music charts anymore. The Puerto Rican superstar has officially stepped into fashion in a big way, partnering with Zara to launch an exclusive clothing collection under his own brand, Benito Antonio. The collab was announced on Zara's Instagram and immediately sent the internet into a frenzy.

Zara is one of the biggest fast-fashion retailers on the planet, owned by Spanish parent company Inditex. Teaming up with Bad Bunny, who has spent years building a reputation as one of the most culturally plugged-in artists alive, is a statement move for both sides. The Benito Antonio brand gives the collection a personal stamp that goes beyond a typical celebrity endorsement.

Bad Bunny has always treated fashion as part of his identity. From his early days pushing gender-fluid style choices to sitting front row at major runway shows, he has made it clear that clothes are not an afterthought for him. The Benito Antonio name signals this is not just a licensing deal. It is his brand, his vision.

The Zara x Benito Antonio collection arrives at a moment when celebrity-driven fashion lines are more competitive than ever. But Bad Bunny bringing his cultural weight and deeply loyal fanbase to Zara gives this one real momentum. Keep your eyes on Zara's channels because pieces from this kind of collab tend to sell out fast.
keyPoints: Bad Bunny has officially partnered with Zara on a clothing collection
The collection is released under his personal brand called Benito Antonio
Zara announced the collaboration on its Instagram
Bad Bunny has long positioned himself as a serious fashion figure

articles.title: Trump White House Posts AI James Bond Image With MAGA Text
articles.summary: The official White House social media account posted an AI-generated image of Donald Trump styled as James Bond, silhouetted in a tuxedo holding a gun on a faux movie poster stamped with 'Make America Great Again.' The post was a direct response to news that Amazon MGM Studios has begun auditions to find the next 007. Internet users wasted no time mocking it, with parody images and joke titles flooding social media within hours.
articles.content:
The White House is no stranger to AI-generated imagery of Donald Trump. Earlier this year Trump posted a picture of himself dressed as the Pope, and in April 2026 he shared a Christ-like image of himself appearing to heal a sick man, which he later deleted after widespread backlash. Now the official White House social media account has taken that self-mythologizing one step further by casting the president as the world's most famous spy.

On Saturday evening, the White House posted an AI image of Donald Trump as a silhouetted James Bond, posed in a tuxedo and holding a gun in the style of a movie poster. The overlay text read 'Make America Great Again.' The post was a direct reaction to a Variety-reported scoop that Amazon MGM Studios has kicked off the search for the next James Bond, with casting director Nina Gold, known for her work on Game of Thrones, The Crown, and Star Wars: The Force Awakens, leading the process to find a successor to Daniel Craig.

There is one pretty obvious problem with the fantasy. As Variety noted, James Bond works for MI6, the United Kingdom's foreign intelligence service, which would immediately disqualify an American president from holding the role. Internet users were quick to point that out and piled on with parody images of their own. One X user captioned a counter-image 'License to Groom.' Another riffed with 'Pie Another Day.' A third posted an AI-generated image of Trump as an overweight Bond holding a Big Mac in place of a pistol, with the caption 'I Don't Care About Your Finances,' a nod to Trump's recent remark about American household finances amid the Iran conflict.

The actual Bond casting story is genuinely exciting for film fans. Amazon MGM Studios locked down creative control over the James Bond franchise in February 2025 after striking a deal with longtime producers Michael G. Wilson and Barbara Broccoli. Director Denis Villeneuve, the filmmaker behind Dune and Arrival, is set to helm the next Bond film. Rumoured frontrunners for the lead role include Jacob Elordi, Callum Turner, and Aaron Taylor-Johnson. Trump, it is safe to say, is not on the shortlist.
keyPoints: The White House posted an AI image of Donald Trump as James Bond on Saturday evening with 'Make America Great Again' text overlaid
The post was a response to news that Amazon MGM Studios has begun casting for the next James Bond film
Casting director Nina Gold, known for Game of Thrones and The Crown, is leading the search for Daniel Craig's successor
Internet users mocked the image widely with parody titles including 'License to Groom' and 'Pie Another Day'
Director Denis Villeneuve is attached to the next Bond film, with Jacob Elordi, Callum Turner, and Aaron Taylor-Johnson among the rumoured candidates

articles.title: South Korean Soldiers Getting Nose Jobs on Leave Are Breaking Military Operations
articles.summary: A surge of Gen Z South Korean soldiers using their leave to get elective cosmetic surgery, from double eyelid procedures to rhinoplasties, is causing real disruptions to front-line military operations. Soldiers are returning to base with unhealed wounds and being excused from training, guard duty, and trench maintenance, forcing their peers to pick up the slack. The Korean military has no formal regulations to deal with it, and clinics are actively targeting soldiers with military discounts.
articles.content:
South Korea is one of the world's leading countries for cosmetic surgery. That culture has now marched straight into the military, and commanders are not happy about it.

A growing trend of active duty South Korean soldiers getting elective cosmetic procedures on leave is creating real operational problems, according to a report originally published in the Korean-language daily Hankook Ilbo. Soldiers are returning to their units after rhinoplasties and double eyelid surgeries with swollen, unhealed faces and being excused from training and duties as a result. One lieutenant, identified as First Lt. Kim, said he was forced to pull a soldier from critical cold-weather training after the soldier came back with a fresh nose job. "I was flustered because he underwent a nose job without saying a word right before the training," Kim said. A second lieutenant in the Chungcheong region had to remove a soldier from night guard duty after the soldier returned with severely swollen eyes from double eyelid surgery.

The surgery trend is being fuelled by a combination of rising military pay, a more relaxed military culture, and aggressive clinic marketing aimed directly at recruits. A consultation manager at a Gangnam-based plastic surgery clinic confirmed that two to three active duty soldiers visit weekly for eye or nose consultations. Clinics are running social media ads offering military discounts and advice on using private health insurance. When a Hankook Ilbo journalist posed as an active duty soldier at a Gangnam clinic, they were offered a 40 percent discount on the spot. One corporal, Cpl. Choi, said he saved his 1.2 million won ($815) monthly salary for several months specifically to afford eyelid surgery.

The problem is made worse because commanders have almost no tools to handle it. The Framework Act on Military Status and Service and unit management directives contain no specific rules about elective surgery recovery. That leaves officers in a bind: excuse the recovering soldier and anger the teammates who have to cover for them, or force a soldier with an unhealed wound into physical duties and risk a medical complication. At least one case involved a soldier's mother calling a commander to request her son be excused from trench maintenance because his eyelid surgery had not healed.

Military studies professor Choi Gi-il of Sangji University says the armed forces need systematic management measures that balance combat readiness with "the values of a changing generation." That is easier said than done. South Korea requires most men to complete roughly 18 to 21 months of mandatory military service, and Gen Z soldiers appear unwilling to put their beauty routines entirely on hold while they serve. Without clear regulations, this culture clash is only going to get louder.
keyPoints: Gen Z South Korean soldiers are getting cosmetic procedures like rhinoplasties and double eyelid surgeries on leave
Soldiers returning with unhealed wounds are being excused from training, guard duty, and trench work
Plastic surgery clinics in Gangnam are offering soldiers military discounts of up to 40 percent
South Korean military law has no specific rules covering elective surgery recovery for active duty troops
Commanders are facing resentment from soldiers forced to cover for recovering peers

articles.title: Kiefer Sutherland Cancels US Tour, Bluntly Blames Low Ticket Sales
articles.summary: Kiefer Sutherland has cancelled the entire US leg of his Love Will Bring You Home Tour, citing "very low ticket sales" in a refreshingly honest Instagram post on May 15. The tour was meant to promote his fourth album, Grey, due out May 29, and would have been his first US shows in seven years. Sutherland joins a growing list of artists including Post Malone, Zayn, Meghan Trainor, and the Pussycat Dolls who have all recently pulled out of US dates.
articles.content:
Kiefer Sutherland is best known as Jack Bauer, the no-nonsense counter-terrorism agent he played for eight seasons on the hit TV series 24. Turns out he brings that same blunt energy to his music career. On May 15, Sutherland posted a statement on Instagram that most artists would never have the nerve to write. "With great disappointment I am going to have to cancel the US leg of my tour due to very low ticket sales," he wrote. No vague health excuse. No "scheduling conflicts." Just the truth.

The cancelled dates were the US leg of his Love Will Bring You Home Tour, which would have kicked off in Patchogue, New York and wrapped in Las Vegas. It would have been his first American shows in seven years. Sutherland had been touring Europe and the UK successfully, but the US numbers simply were not there. In his statement, he added, "I don't think it's fair to the people who have bought tickets, or the venues, to play to half empty houses." Refunds are available through the original point of purchase.

The tour was built around his fourth studio album, Grey, which is still set for release on May 29. Sutherland has described Grey as his most personal record yet, saying the songs "mark a growth for me personally, as I wrote less about observations and more about personal feelings" he has been working through in his own life. The album was written and recorded over a two-year period from 2024 to 2026.

Sutherland is not alone in this. The live music industry in the US is in a rough patch right now, and the phenomenon has a nickname: "blue dot fever," a reference to the sea of unsold blue dots that appear on Ticketmaster listings when a show is not moving tickets. Post Malone, Meghan Trainor, Zayn, and the Pussycat Dolls have all recently cancelled or scaled back US tour dates. Industry observers point to a toxic mix of sky-high ticket prices, a post-pandemic hangover, and a simply oversaturated concert market. Pollstar data shows ticket prices surged 41.3 percent between 2019 and 2024, and fans are increasingly pushing back.

What makes Sutherland's situation stand out is not the cancellation itself but how he handled it. Most artists hide behind vague statements. Sutherland just told his fans the truth, promised them refunds, and said he hopes to return "at a more suitable time." In a PR landscape full of spin, that kind of straight talk is genuinely rare. And honestly? It might do more for his reputation than a half-empty US tour ever would have.
keyPoints: Kiefer Sutherland cancelled the US leg of his Love Will Bring You Home Tour on May 15 due to very low ticket sales
The cancelled dates would have been his first US shows in seven years
His fourth album, Grey, is still releasing on May 29 as planned
Sutherland joins Post Malone, Zayn, Meghan Trainor, and the Pussycat Dolls in recent US tour cancellations
His unusually honest public statement was widely praised for skipping the usual vague excuses

articles.title: Ronaldo's First Al-Nassr Trophy Slips Away Again After Gamba Osaka Upset
articles.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo's long wait for a major trophy with Al-Nassr goes on after the Saudi club were beaten 1-0 at home by Gamba Osaka in the AFC Champions League Two final. It is a stinging loss for one of football's greatest ever scorers, beaten in their own stadium by Japanese opposition. The defeat will only intensify questions about whether Ronaldo will ever lift silverware during his time in Saudi Arabia.
articles.content:
It was supposed to be the night Cristiano Ronaldo finally got his hands on a major trophy with Al-Nassr. Instead, Gamba Osaka came to Riyadh and left with the AFC Champions League Two title after a 1-0 win that nobody saw coming.

The AFC Champions League Two is the second-tier continental club competition in Asian football, sitting below the main AFC Champions League Elite. For Al-Nassr, it represented a genuine shot at ending what has become an increasingly uncomfortable trophy drought for the club and its biggest star.

Ronaldo joined Al-Nassr in January 2023 after leaving Manchester United, and the promise of silverware was always part of the sell. Since then, the club has come close but never quite got there. Losing a home final to a Japanese side makes this one hurt even more.

Gamba Osaka's 1-0 victory is a proper sporting upset. Al-Nassr had home advantage and were widely expected to win. The result is a reminder that football does not care about reputations, and for Ronaldo, the wait goes on.
keyPoints: Al-Nassr lost 1-0 to Gamba Osaka in the AFC Champions League Two final
The match was played at Al-Nassr's home ground, making the loss even more painful
Ronaldo still has no major trophy since joining Al-Nassr in January 2023
Gamba Osaka pulled off a genuine upset against one of Asia's wealthiest clubs

articles.title: Yami International Film Festival Brings Horror to Tokyo in November
articles.summary: The Yami International Film Festival, a brand-new horror and dark genre festival, will make its debut November 20-26, 2026 at Human Trust Cinema Shibuya in Tokyo. Organizers from Japan-based Yami Inc. announced the festival at Cannes, with submissions opening May 27 via FilmFreeway. Yami takes its name from the Japanese word for darkness and carries the tagline "Darkness Makes the World Known."
articles.content:
Horror fans in Tokyo are getting their own film festival. The Yami International Film Festival has announced its inaugural edition, running November 20 to 26, 2026 at Human Trust Cinema Shibuya in the heart of Tokyo. The news dropped at Cannes, where Yami Inc., the Japan-based horror entertainment company behind the event, made the announcement to the global film industry.

The name Yami comes from the Japanese word for darkness, which pretty much tells you everything you need to know about the vibe. The festival's tagline is "Darkness Makes the World Known" and it is built specifically for horror and dark genre cinema. That is a focused mandate in a world where most festivals try to cover everything. Yami is going all in on one lane.

The festival will run two competitive sections: a feature film competition and a short film competition. Prizes will include best film, an audience award, and additional honors, with winners taking home both cash prizes and official trophies. The jury roster has yet to be announced but will draw from filmmakers, actors, and creators in the genre space. Submissions open on May 27 through FilmFreeway, so genre filmmakers around the world can start putting their entries together right now.

The timing is sharp. Japan is having a major cultural moment in global cinema in 2026, with the country named as the Country of Honor at the Cannes Marché du Film this year. Japanese horror has long had a global fanbase, from classics like Ringu to more recent festival darlings, and a dedicated international horror festival based in Tokyo feels like a natural next step. Human Trust Cinema Shibuya is a well-known arthouse venue, which suggests Yami is pitching itself at serious cinephiles rather than pure genre thrill-seekers. Watch this one grow.
keyPoints: The Yami International Film Festival debuts November 20-26, 2026 in Tokyo
Organized by Yami Inc., it focuses exclusively on horror and dark genre cinema
Two competitive sections: feature film and short film, with cash prizes and trophies
Submissions open May 27 via FilmFreeway
The festival name means "darkness" in Japanese and is announced against the backdrop of Japan's big year at Cannes

articles.title: Balotelli and Douglas Costa Are Saving Chievo Verona From Oblivion
articles.summary: Chievo Verona, once a celebrated Serie A club, are fighting for promotion from Serie D with an unlikely squad that includes Mario Balotelli and former Brazil winger Douglas Costa. It is a wild footnote in two very different careers, and a genuinely heart-warming story about a fallen club trying to climb back. If they get promoted, it will be one of the feel-good football stories of the year.
articles.content:
Chievo Verona used to play in Serie A, Italy's top flight, and even competed in European football in the early 2000s. Fast forward to 2026 and the club are scrapping it out in Serie D, which is the fourth tier of Italian football. It is a long way to fall, and clawing back up is never easy.

But Chievo Verona have found some unlikely allies. Mario Balotelli, the wildly talented and endlessly controversial Italian striker who has played for the likes of Manchester City, Inter Milan, and the Italian national team, has signed up to help the cause. So has Douglas Costa, the Brazilian winger who spent years at Juventus and Bayern Munich and earned caps for the Brazil national team.

Two players with careers that took them to the very top of the game are now lacing up their boots in the fourth tier of Italian football to help a historic club survive. It sounds like the setup for a movie, but it is real life.

For Balotelli, who has always been a polarising figure, this is a chance to do something that actually makes people feel good. For Douglas Costa, it is a homecoming of sorts to the sport he loves at a level where every game genuinely matters. Both men know what it means to play in front of big crowds at big clubs. Now they are finding out what it means to fight for promotion from the very bottom.

Chievo Verona's story is about more than football. It is about a community trying to reclaim something it lost. And with two big names in their corner, the dream of climbing back through the Italian football pyramid suddenly feels a little more real.
keyPoints: Chievo Verona are competing in Serie D, the fourth tier of Italian football
Mario Balotelli and ex-Brazil winger Douglas Costa have joined the club's promotion push
Chievo were once a Serie A club and played in European competition
The duo bring serious top-level pedigree to a very grassroots fight
Promotion from Serie D would be a major milestone in the club's rebuild
